scenarios.scenario-1: `/api/request` accepts a valid email and returns an accepted response without leaking issuance details.
scenarios.scenario-2: `/verify` consumes a valid token and returns the final proxy result page.
scenarios.scenario-3: Invalid email, malformed token, expired token, or replay are rejected with stable status mapping and safe body content.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: Token consumption is one-time from the HTTP edge perspective.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Error bodies remain human-readable without exposing secrets or internals.

side-effects.effect-1: Creates tokens, triggers email delivery, and initiates proxy issuance.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Mask or hash emails in logs.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Never log full token values or proxy secrets.

verification-levels.wave-level: Request-to-verify integration using fake Resend and token store.
verification-levels.phase-level: Public HTTPS flow through portal and issuance path.

production-gates.gate-1: Expired, malformed, and replayed verification paths never render a false success page.
production-gates.gate-2: Operator-facing logs distinguish request validation, dispatch, consume, and render phases.
